After a couple of hours catching pin whiting at broad beach I got on my mark for <hide>.<hide>am and had thornies on my first <hide> casts before the dogs came out to play...had one more ray at the death but the night will be remembered for the massive huss I almost landed before it pulled the hook as I tried to pull it up the rocky ramp....my pb is just short of <hide> lb and this thing would have ate it for breakfast....cracking night with no wind or swell....full squid baits were best.
Good session Kev.
Unlucky on the Huss.
Crackin session kev, Hard lines on the huss laaa.
Well done Kev laaaa, been itchin to get over there for a few weeks, unlucky on the Huss.

I finished my homework at school this week.
Busy at school and sometimes come here at weekends.I'm willing to be your friend and you can e-mail me.
Got some of my stuff moved out of the flat today.
It actually genuinely relaxes me to get down to the business of moving.
Maybe it's just that it's taking me closer to the day when I never have to see that wanker's face again.
mangaluva wrote: Got some of my stuff moved out of the flat today.
It actually genuinely relaxes me to get down to the business of moving.
Maybe it's just that it's taking me closer to the day when I never have to see that wanker's face again.
Although I'm still not going back to the flat until they have told him to sod off and he has done so.
Comic-con was a great weekend away.
Good times were had by the whole society.
Best parts included meeting David Lloyd, who is a very funny and friendly man, getting a hug from Paul McGann, getting freaking adorable manga portraits drawn by Yishan Li and an improptu rugby game with a plushie at the train station ("Highlanders versus the Foreign Fucks").
Also, swords.
Like all of us bought swords.
It was awesome.
